releases.name: Add legacy encryption driver for backward compatibility
Breaking - Remove serialize method from HTTP context
releases.description:
# [7.0.0-next.23](https://github.com/adonisjs/core/compare/v7.0.0-next.22...v7.0.0-next.23) (2026-01-10)

Since most of the projects building APIs would like to reformat the response returned by the API, we cannot ship with a static serialize method from the core. Instead, the starter kit will include an APISerializer with some defaults that can be overridden within the user-land.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/core/compare/v7.0.0-next.22...v7.0.0-next.23

releases.name: New encryption module, client manifest file generation and ability to add/configure multiple packages
releases.description:
# [7.0.0-next.15](/compare/v7.0.0-next.14...v7.0.0-next.15) (2025-12-15)


### Bug Fixes

* test utils cookie client creation 30473d4


### Features

* add support for multiple packages in `add` (#5021) a4149ed, closes #5021
* data output file to reference client manifest 22ffa1f
* generate config and adonisrc.ts manifest file in frontend types 294266c
* integrate boringnode/encryption package c5f5e7f
* only include the auth file inside the frontend manifest a1202bf


### BREAKING CHANGES

* The container binding encryption now returns an instance
of the EncryptionManager and not the Encryption class. This is because we now
allow for multiple Encryptors to exist in a project.

Also removed `appKey` in favor of dedicated `config/encryption.ts` file

## What's Changed
* feat: add support for multiple packages in add by @Julien-R44 in https://github.com/adonisjs/core/pull/5021


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/core/compare/v7.0.0-next.14...v7.0.0-next.15

releases.name: Add support for dd and dump helpers
releases.description:
The `dd` helper can be used like `console.log` but it provides a better visual experience and also comes with a HTML printer.  The helper throws an exception and self handles itself to render the dumped value inside the browser (during an HTTP request) or in the console (default output).

You can use the `dd` helper as follows.

```ts
import { dd } from '@adonisjs/core/services/dd'
dd(someValue)
```

The same helper is available in Edge template as `@dd` tag. Also, within Edge templates you may use the `@dump` to inspect a value without throwing an exception.

```edge
{{-- Dump and halt rendering --}}
@dd(someValue)

{{-- Dump and continue rendering --}}
@dump(someValue)
```

## Bug Fixes

* handle case where stdout.columns can be undefined ([437cb2d](https://github.com/adonisjs/core/commit/437cb2d0ecdb8742e90b19ea3d334c2b1a16be7c))
* handle case where whitespace is a negative value ([638c457](https://github.com/adonisjs/core/commit/638c4571142250d4626bada9d97e3003f43dce48))
* handle NaN value case ([619a3a8](https://github.com/adonisjs/core/commit/619a3a81c40032218b7f757e991cb299f94649a5))
* use poppinss/colors as styleText is not widely supported ([2a491ec](https://github.com/adonisjs/core/commit/2a491ecd57bdad58c8b0dda2548de0a6597181f2))


## Features

* add dumper and upgrade edge.js with new stacks functionality ([f6e05ba](https://github.com/adonisjs/core/commit/f6e05ba1669d5351fc7aa7a72a98e7f56f0dac72))
* improve dd output and add edge plugin ([9b18e6a](https://github.com/adonisjs/core/commit/9b18e6aed2f5e22e8d41a6f220dc81781778d970))

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/core/compare/v6.13.1...v6.14.0

releases.name: Preparing for v6 of AdonisJS
releases.description:
This release of Ace is a complete rewrite in ESM. The framework is a lot simpler, has a better CLI arguments parser, and is built with testing in mind.

The newer version will enable you to write tests for almost every action you perform inside a command.

* Test if your command writes a log to `stdout` or `stderr`.
* Test if the log message label color is green and the message is gray.
* Test if your command prints a table with three columns.
* Test if your command displays a prompt and uses the prompt return value.

I think you get the idea :)

## Breaking changes
This release has a lot of breaking changes. Let’s go over them one by one.

> **Warning**: Release notes for AdonisJS apps will be published when releasing v6. Since AdonisJS apps do not use all the ace APIs, the impact of breaking changes will be minor.

### ESM only
The newer versions of Ace will not work with CommonJS projects. Ace is now ESM only.

### Reorganized imports
The imports have been reorganized using the subpath exports map of Node.js.

* All TypeScript types are exported from `@adonisjs/ace/types`.
* All runtime code is exported from `@adonisjs/ace`

The entry point of the package has different exports as well.

```diff
export {
   Kernel,
   args,
   flags,
   BaseCommand,
-  handlError,
-  ManifestLoader,
-  ManifestGenerator,
-  listDirectoryFiles,
+  Parser,
+  errors,
+  HelpCommand,
+  ListCommand,
+  FsLoader,
+  ListLoader,
+  ExceptionHandler,
+  IndexGenerator,
}
```

### Parser changes

We have replaced the underlying CLI parser from [getopts](https://npm.im/getopts) with [yargs-parser](https://npm.im/yargs-parser). Yargs CLI parser is more accurate and has more features.

We do not expect you to see any breaking changes because of the parser swap because we used the parser under the hood and defined the command properties from the parsed output.

So as long as you reference user input values from the command properties, i.e., `this.name` or `this.resource`, all should be good. 

However, if you were reading properties from `this.parsed`, you will have to consult the new output and make modifications. 

### Base command

* Rename static property `settings` to `options`.
* Rename static method `$addArgument` to `defineArgument`. The method signature is changed as well. The method now accepts the name as the first parameter and the options as the 2nd parameter.
* Rename static method `$addFlag` to `defineFlag`. The method signature is changed as well. The method now accepts the name as the first parameter and the options as the 2nd parameter.
* Rename method `exit` with `terminate`.
* Remove method `onExit`. Instead, use the `application.terminating` hook inside your commands (AdonisJS only)
* Remove property `generator`. Instead, use the `application.stubs` property to scaffold files from templates.
* Remove property `isInteractive`. I do not know of any instance of us using it.

### Decorators
The command arguments and flags are defined using the `args` and the `flags` decorator.

The following example defines an argument for the `name` and a flag called `createResource` . We use the `name` option to override the names accepted via the command line.

For example, in the command line, we will set the value of `createResource` using the `—resource` flag.

After this release, we have renamed the `name` property to `argumentName` and `flagName`

```diff
args.string({
- name: 'controller-name',
+ argumentName: 'controller-name'
})
public name: string

flags.boolean({
- name: 'resource'
+ flagName: 'resource'
})
public createResource: boolean
```

* Remove the `numArray` flag. Instead, define an array flag and cast values to numbers.

### Kernel
Kernel API sees a major overhaul because of how commands are now registered. The earlier kernel was responsible for providing multiple ways to load commands. That included using manifest JSON files and registering commands directly.

Now, all these responsibilities have been shifted to new loaders API. In an AdonisJS project, you might have never interacted with the old kernel API, so you might not have to change much in your apps anyways.

* Remove the `isInteractive` getter
* Remove `isMockingConsoleOutput`. Mocking is now handled by the TUI library directly.
* Replace the `defaultCommand`  property with the `getDefaultCommand` command.
* Replace the `commands` property with the `getCommands` method. The method returns an array of commands sorted by the command name.
* Replace the `aliases` property with the `getAliases` method.
* Remove the `error` property. You can read it from the command directly.
* Replace the old hooks system with new hooks (check the hooks section for the same)
* Remove the `register` method. Instead, use loaders to register commands.
* Replace the `flag` method with the `defineFlag` method. The method API has also changed. The `flag` handler is not defined inline; instead, you must use the `on` method to listen for global and command flags.
* Remove the `useManifest` method. Instead, use loaders API
* Remove the `onExit` method. Instead, the commands can hook into the `application.terminating` hook to know if an AdonisJS application will terminate.
* Rename `getSuggestions` to `getCommandSuggestions`.
* Remove `preloadManifest`. Instead, use loaders API
* The `find` method now accepts the command name. Earlier it used to accept an array of CLI `args`.
* Remove the `runDefaultCommand` method. Exposing this method has no purpose.
* Remove the `mockConsoleOutput` method. To mock the logger calls, you can use `kernel.ui.switchMode('raw')`. This will collect loggers in memory and turn off colors as well.
* Remove the `printHelp` method. The `HelpCommand` can be used instead.
* Remove the `exit` method. Call `app.terminate` on the AdonisJS application instance instead.

## Hooks
* The `before` and `after` hooks have been removed in favor of descriptive methods like `finding`, `executing`,`loading`, and so on.

Following are the changes you must make in your codebase if listening for kernel hooks.

```diff
-kernel.before('run', () => {
-})

+ kernel.executing(() => {
+ })
```

```diff
-kernel.before('find', () => {
-})

+ kernel.finding(() => {
+ })
```

```diff
-kernel.after('run', () => {
-})

+ kernel.executed(() => {
+ })
```

```diff
-kernel.after('find', () => {
-})

+ kernel.loading(() => {
+ })
```

## Commits

- feat: add isMain property to base command  ade6a2a
- feat: add exception handler  e35bc4e
- fix: normalize file relative paths to have unix slash  406c14a
- test: fix breaking tests  dfb4091
- refactor: remove module and add toolkit commands  d0816b0
- refactor: cleanup modules loader source  99c10e9
- feat: add importPath to command metadata import using FsLoader  1580a1a
- refactor: remove template methods prepare, interact and completed  7992de9
- fix: use declare modifier for defining property names  1ed81fc
- feat: remove termination logic from kernel  88ea74e
- feat: make kernel accept any sort of base command  471f25f
- feat: use json schema to validate commands loaded by fs and modules loader  f7094e4
- feat: add support for prompts  4e65670
- fix: casing of decorators directory  7fdef55
- test: normalize test import path to a file URL  217a1d9
- feat: add FsLoader for loading commands from a directory  ab8a2a1
- refactor: rename CommandsList to ListLoader  48442d5
- feat: add modules loader to load commands from ES modules  ec115f8
- docs(README): update documentation  50d3d76

## What's Changed

releases.name: Prevent mass assignment vulnerability
releases.description:
Security update for [CVE-2026-22814](https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid/security/advisories/GHSA-g5gc-h5hp-555f).

This release fixes a **mass assignment vulnerability** in Lucid that could allow user input to override internal ORM state properties.

The issue was caused by relying on `hasOwnProperty` checks during model assignments, which unintentionally allowed setting internal properties like `$attributes`, `$original`, or `$isPersisted` when passing **untrusted input** to methods such as `fill`, `merge`, or `create`.

Applications that already **properly validate and whitelist input data before passing it to Lucid models are not affected**.

releases.name: Prevent mass assignment vulnerability
releases.description:
Security update for [CVE-2026-22814](https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid/security/advisories/GHSA-g5gc-h5hp-555f).

This release fixes a **mass assignment vulnerability** in Lucid that could allow user input to override internal ORM state properties.

The issue was caused by relying on `hasOwnProperty` checks during model assignments, which unintentionally allowed setting internal properties like `$attributes`, `$original`, or `$isPersisted` when passing **untrusted input** to methods such as `fill`, `merge`, or `create`.

Applications that already **properly validate and whitelist input data before passing it to Lucid models are not affected**.

releases.name: Support for libSQL and new health checks
releases.description:
This release adds first-class support for libSQL and removes all options + types + methods related to legacy way of doing health checks. Instead, you must use the following two classes to register health checks with AdonisJS v6.

```ts
import db from '@adonisjs/lucid/services/db'
import { DbConnectionCountCheck, DbCheck } from '@adonisjs/lucid/database'

new HealthChecks().register([
  new DbCheck(db.connection()),
  new DbConnectionCountCheck(db.connection())
])
```

## Breaking changes

- The `config.healthCheck` property
- Remove `connection.getReport` method.
- Remove `connectionManager.report` method.
- Remove `ReportNode` type.

## Deprecations

- Deprecate `connection.dialectName` in favor of `connection.clientName`. The `clientName` refers to the npm clients you can use with Knex and `dialectName` refers to the Lucid dialects. One dialect can be used with multiple clients. For example, the MySQL dialect can be used with `mysql` and `mysql2` clients.

## Commits

- test: another attempt at fixing broken tests  8a4df1c
- test: fix breaking tests  2d9697b
- refactor: update usages of dialectName with clientName  7fedbfe
- feat: add support for libsql and cleanup clients to dialects mapping  95f11d1
- feat: export health check classes  1b32dca
- feat: add database health checks and remove legacy health check flag  0373e7f
- refactor: remove legacy health checks  32f377e
- test: fix broken types  44da18c
- chore: update peer dependencies  9dbd7fc
- chore: update dependencies  bdc32b5

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid/compare/v20.6.0...v21.0.0

releases.name: Improvements to the DateTime comparison when using "orCreate" methods
releases.description:
In this release, we improve the comparison logic used by `firstOrCreate`, `firstOrNew`, and `updateOrCreate` methods to properly compare Luxon DateTime instances when finding or persisting a new row.

releases.name: Bug fixes and new API on BaseModel
releases.description:
This release fixes the `dropAllTables` implementation for SQLite so that we can drop tables even when unsafe mode is disabled.

Also, a new model method `lockForUpdate` has been added. This method refetches the model instance from the database and locks the row for update. This allows you to perform updates without running into race-conditions. For example:

```ts
// Assuming you already have a model instance.

const user = auth.user.lockForUpdate((newUser) => {
  // newUser is a fresh model instance and now row is locked inside the database
  newUser.email = newEmail
  await newUser.save()
  return newUser
})
```

## Commits

- test: reset tables between tests  1b47ba7
- feat: add model.lockForUpdate method to lock the model row for updates  703e6b5
- chore(package): update dependencies  018c4ce
- refactor: change sqlite dropAllTables implementation (#1001)  5c18c76

## What's Changed
* refactor: change sqlite dropAllTables implementation by @Julien-R44 in https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid/pull/1001

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid/compare/v20.2.0...v20.3.0

releases.name: Switch default naming strategy to camelCase and add support for nulls treatment in order by clause
releases.description:
## Breaking change

- In this release we switch the naming strategy of the ORM and the Paginator to return camelCase output for the serialized object keys. This should have made its way in the last release, but somehow missed it. You can switch back to SnakeCase strategy by explicitly assigning it to the BaseModel. https://lucid.adonisjs.com/docs/model-naming-strategy

### Switching back to `snake_case` naming strategy
If you like to keep using the old snake case naming strategy, then you can copy-paste the following code inside a service provider of your application.

```ts
import { SnakeCaseNamingStrategy } from '@adonisjs/lucid/orm'
import { BaseModel } from '@adonisjs/lucid/orm'

export default class AppProvider {
  async boot() {
     BaseModel.namingStrategy = new SnakeCaseNamingStrategy()
  }
}
```

## New features

Add support for defining treatment of null values in `orderBy` method. For example:

```ts
orderBy([
  { column: 'expires_at', order: 'desc', nulls: 'last' },
  { column: 'last_used_at', order: 'desc', nulls: 'first' }
])
```

The above method calls will add compile to `ORDER BY expires_at desc NULLS LAST` and `ORDER BY last_used_at desc NULLS FIRST` sql statements

## Commits

- feat: add support for nulls property for orderby method signature  b4af593
- chore(package): update dependencies  4777edc
- chore: remove `@types/pluralize` from dependencies  cee062c
- Merge pull request #986 from adonisjs/feat/serialize_to_camelcase  d33b9b1
- refactor: use camelCase naming strategy with paginator also  7e35cc2
- fix: remove unused imports  d327871
- feat: change naming strategy to output camelCase key names in serialized output  c835376
- chore: update dependencies  718c68f
- fix(seeds): correct log for ignored seed  71708b8

## What's Changed
* Breaking change - Switch ORM naming strategy to CamelCase by @thetutlage in https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid/pull/986
* chore: remove `@types/pluralize` from dependencies by @targos in https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid/pull/987

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid/compare/v19.0.0...v20.0.0

releases.name: Migrate to work with AdonisJS v6 core
releases.description:
This release updates the Lucid codebase to work with AdonisJS v6. There are minor breaking changes in the API of the ORM, alongside the ESM migration.

## Breaking changes

- The package is ESM only.
- The `@ioc` style import paths have been removed in favor of standard imports. The final AdonisJS migration guide will contain the new import paths.
- The deprecated `developmentOnly` flag has been removed from seeders. Use `environment` property instead.
- Rename `table` CLI flag in `make:migration` command to `alter`. 
- The `alter` and `create` CLI flags in `make:migration` command now accept boolean values and act as an action.

That is all!

## Commits

- chore: update dependencies and peer dependencies  c9d28af
- docs: update README  98d74e9
- ci: update typecheck job's command  e50c45d
- fix: path for sqlite and mssql db password  f41bd17

releases.name: Support import.meta.hot
releases.description:
## Changes

We have a cache system for the Module Importer and Module Expressions to avoid re-importing the same file several times. 

Now, if ever `import.meta.hot` is defined, then this cache will not be used and the file constantly re-imported, allowing the use of Hot module reloading libraries like [Hot-Hook](https://github.com/julien-R44/hot-hook). See https://github.com/adonisjs/fold/pull/62

## Commits

* chore: update dependencies (468df8f)
* chore: migrate to release-it (e67f821)
* refactor: keep conditionals checks inline (4efabc8)
* feat: import.meta.hot support (bfe649e)

releases.name: Add support for swapping implementations
releases.description:
The goal of the `container.swap` method is to allow swapping implementations during testing. This is not same as the previous fakes API. Earlier, we were using Proxies to fake pre-resolved objects as well. The entire mechanism was flaky and yet never documented.

The `container.swap` is a very simple in nature and only works when you are using DI and not with static container services.

Please check the README for the usage documentation.

## Commits

- feat: add support for swaps  be5e23e

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/fold/compare/v9.0.0-0...v9.0.1-0

releases.name: Preparing for the next major release (ESM Only)
releases.description:
This release of `@adonisjs/fold` simplifies the Container a lot. The code size is smaller, the logic is more straightforward, and many responsibilities have been removed from the container.

> **Warning**: This is a pre-release version tweaked for the upcoming major release of AdonisJS that will be ESM only.

> **Note**: None of these changes impact the end-user applications written in AdonisJS. These APIs are used internally except for a few, like `resolveBinding` (used by all the packages). The `@inject` decorator and the `make` methods are largely unchanged.

At a high level, the following simplifications have been made.

- Node with ES modules and sub-path imports does a lot of work for the container. For example, we do not have to invent the import aliasing at our end. [Node.js sub-path imports](https://nodejs.org/dist/latest-v18.x/docs/api/packages.html#subpath-imports) does it for us.
- Removed all the normalization code written around the `esModuleInterop` flag of TypeScript.
- Removed fakes from the container. They were never documented and neither used. Also, this results in not using Proxies at all. We required proxies to make fakes work.
- Replace half-baked traps with a dedicated hooks system.
- Make all APIs async by default. No need to have two flavors for every method. So, for example, `make,` and `makeAsync` is now `make`.

## API changes

- Remove the `trap` method. Use the `container.resolving` hook instead.
- Remove `getResolver` method. Use `container.createResolver` instead. The resolver has different responsibilities altogether now.
- Merge `callAsync` and `call` to be `call`.
- Merge `makeAsync` and `make` to be `make`.
- Remove the `withBindings` method in favor of the `resolving` hook. The semantics of both methods are different. However, we mainly used the `withBindings` method to resolve and extend bindings. This method eagerly loaded all the bindings. The `resolving` hook can serve the same purpose, but lazily.
- Remove `useAsync` and `use` methods. Just use `make`. We added these methods to transform the `@ioc:` imports to function calls. We will be using container services in the future.
- Remove the `require` method, as we do not have an alias now. Instead, we use the Node sub-paths imports and, therefore, can call `import(alias)` directly.
- Remove the `import` method. The method was the same as `require` but was for async module loading.
- Remove the `resolveBinding` method. Just use `make`.
- Remove `lookup` and `lookupOrFail`. We used these methods to find which kind of binding are trying to resolve. Is it a pure binding or an alias? Since we do not have many branches, we no longer need the `lookup` method.
- Remove `isAliasPath` and `alias`, since there are no aliases.
- Remove `fake`, `hasFake` and `restore` methods. There is no concept of fakes anymore in the container. If you are confused with the fake API documented in the AdonisJS docs, then they are not using the container.
- Remove `useProxies` method. It was required for fakes.

## Resolver API changes

The resolver now has all the container methods except those used for binding values. In other words, the resolver has the complete API to `make` bindings and `call` methods.

- Remove `resolve` in favor of `make`.

## Notes for package maintainers
If you are maintaining a package, you must make the following changes inside the service providers. 

### Register the bindings as earlier
With one simple change, you can register the bindings as you have been doing so far. Instead of using the `resolveBinding` method, you must use the `make` method. For example

```diff
import type { Application } from '@ioc:Adonis/Core/Application'

export default class MyProvider {
  constructor(protected application: Application) {}

  register() {
    this.application.container.singleton('My/Binding', async (resolver) => {
-        const Config = this.application.container.resolveBinding('Adonis/Core/Config')
+        const config = await resolver.make('Adonis/Core/Config')
      return new MyClass(config.get('my_package_config'))
    })
  }
}
```

### Extend using the `resolving` hook
Suppose you used the `boot` lifecycle hook to extend existing bindings. For example: Adding a rule to the `validator` or adding a `macro` to some class, you can do the same using the `resolving` hook.

The `resolving` hook will improve the framework's boot time performance. Because this hook runs only when someone resolves a binding from the container, your code to extend will also run at that time.

```diff
import type { Application } from '@ioc:Adonis/Core/Application'

export default class MyProvider {
  constructor(protected application: Application) {}

  async boot() {
-      this.application.container.withBindings(['Adonis/Core/Validator', 'My/Binding'], (validator, myBinding) => {
-        // code to extend
-      })
    
+      this.application.container.resolving('Adonis/Core/Validator', async (validator, resolver) => {
+        await resolver.make('My/Binding')
+        // code to extend
+      })
  }
}
```

I would not suspect any other breaking changes for the package maintainers unless you were using some internal APIs. Therefore, feel free to let me know, and I will document them here.

## Commits

- style: format code  70b11d0
- feat: add hasBinding and hasAllBindings methods  d2a51ca
- chore: publish under next tag  1129012
- feat: add inject decorator  f9f4582
- refactor: code cleanup  7d904f4
- chore: add public api exports  c4e9087
- feat: add hooks and events support  22908ab
- feat: finish initial implementation  749a607
- docs(README): small improvements  920a735
- docs(README): move badges to the top  ba07afe

releases.name: Add mjml component
releases.description:
The mjml component can be used as `@mjml` tag and it uses the [mjml](https://npmjs.com/mjml) npm package to convert MJML markup to HTML. 

You can use it follows inside within the Edge templates.

```edge
@mjml()
  <mjml>
    <mj-body>
      <mj-section>
        <mj-column>
          <mj-text>
            Hello World!
          </mj-text>
        </mj-column>
      </mj-section>
    </mj-body>
  </mjml>
@end
```

The output will be HTML. Just make sure to install `mjml` package within your application before using the component tag

## Commits

- feat: add mjml component to process mjml markup  3ad4e4b

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/mail/compare/v9.0.0...v9.1.0

releases.name: Final release with major changes
releases.description:
Alright, so from now on, the Mail package might not see any other big refactor. So, let's jot down the breaking changes from `@adonisjs/mail@8.x` to `@adonisjs/mail@9.x`.

## Breaking changes

- The package is pure ESM and hence won't work with CJS projects anymore.
- The `@ioc:Adonis/Addons/Mail` import has been replace with `@adonisjs/mail/services/main`. Both these imports returns an instance of the `MailManager` class.
- Remove `MailManager.extend` method. We have changed the process of creating mail transports completely and the new docs will reflect the same.
- The drivers have been renamed to transports. Since that sounds better and not generic.
- Removed `Mail.monitorQueue` method. Instead listen for the `queued:mail:error` and `mail:sent` events.
- Removed `Mail.scheduleEmail`. The method was never documented
- Renamed `Mailer.driver` property to `Mailer.transport`. The mailer instance is returned when you call `Mail.use` method.
- The FakeMailer has been revamped completely and the new APIs are not compatible with the old one's. So yes, you will have to refactor your tests interacting with the Fake mailer.

That's pretty much all. There are many internal refactors, which should not impact the end-user applications. 

## Commits

- fix: broken path of file attachment test  26314a6
- ci: update TEST_EMAILS_CC env var  81c7bcb
- ci: update env variables  9ad12ce
- feat: add back brevo transport  3923657
- refactor: rename drivers to transports  6eb6ee4
- feat: add support for sending variables to mailgun API call  10be8b2
- feat: add support for list headers  bb13767
- refactor: another streak of refactors  ad15bad

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/mail/compare/v9.0.0-6...v9.0.0-7

releases.name: Configure basic auth guard and export authFinder mixin from its own subpath
releases.description:
The `@adonisjs/auth` can be used with Lucid if someone creates a guard that does not use Lucid under the hood. 

However, we were exporting the `withAuthFinder` mixin from the main entrypoint of the package and this mixin relies on Lucid. Therefore Lucid becomes a hard dependency of the Auth package.

After this release, we have moved the mixin to its own sub module and you can import it as follows.

```diff
- import { withAuthFinder } from '@adonisjs/auth'
+ import { withAuthFinder } from '@adonisjs/auth/mixins/lucid'
```

## Commits

- refactor: export Lucid mixins from its own subpath with descriptive name  15d3761
- Merge pull request #243 from parker-codes/chore/add-basic-auth-guard  c61ccb1
- Merge pull request #239 from adonisjs/fix/hard-dependency  d53cb84
- chore: add Basic auth guard  c58e683
- fix(mixins): update deprecation status  f8a5715
- fix(mixins): ensure we do not lose type of withAuthFinder  96663d3
- fix(mixins): rewrite broken imports  19d7042
- fix(mixins): add mixins file to tsup entry  e4df074
- fix(mixins): remove withAuthFinder from root export  5d04dde

## What's Changed
* fix(mixins): remove withAuthFinder from root export by @RomainLanz in https://github.com/adonisjs/auth/pull/239
* chore: add Basic auth guard by @parker-codes in https://github.com/adonisjs/auth/pull/243

## New Contributors
* @parker-codes made their first contribution in https://github.com/adonisjs/auth/pull/243

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/auth/compare/v9.1.1...v9.2.0

releases.name: Cleanup and breaking changes
releases.description:
This release contains a few breaking changes. Let's talk about them

- We no longer emit events to the AdonisJS global emitter. These events were never documented and neither used.
- The RedisManager no longer has methods to run redis commands. You will have to always get a connection instance and then execute redis commands. 
    ```ts
    // Earlier
    import Redis from '@ioc:Adonis/Addons/Redis'
    Redis.set('key', value)

    // Now
    import redis from '@adonisjs/redis/services/main'
    redis.connection().set('key', value)
    ```
    
    We might consider adding back redis commands to the RedisManager class before the final release, just to reduce the surface area of 
    breaking changes. However, right now the goal is to ship something simple and clean.

- No health checks for now. They will be added before the final public release

## Commits

- refactor: add debug logs  dc1c6da
- refactor: abstracts errors  8512c87
- refactor: merge pubsub methods collection with io methods  311ae4a
- refactor: cleaning things up  97837d7

https://github.com/adonisjs/redis/compare/v8.0.0-3...v8.0.0-4

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/redis/compare/v8.0.0-3...v8.0.0-4

releases.name: Add missing ioredis methods
releases.description:
## Changes

Before this release, we kept a hard-coded string list of ioredis method names and inject them in our wrapper classes. 
As a result, many were missing from this list, and could become out-of-sync with the ioredis API. This caused this kind of issue :

- https://github.com/adonisjs/redis/issues/52
- https://github.com/adonisjs/redis/issues/46

Now we are using the Reflect API to get all the IoRedis methods

## Commits

- chore: update readme  c479ccf

releases.name: Add support for defining and running lua scripts as command
releases.description:
## Define command

```ts
import Redis from '@ioc:Adonis/Addons/Redis'

Redis.defineCommand('defineValue', {
  numberOfKeys: 1,
  lua: `redis.call('set', KEYS[1], ARGV[1])`,
})

await Redis.runCommand('defineValue', 'greeting', 'hello world')
```


- feat: add support for defining custom lua commands  2ddc054

releases.name: Resolve otherField from translations
releases.description:
Certain validation rules like `notSameAs` target other field's value for the validation. In case of failure, the `otherField` name is encoded in the default error message. However, before this release the `otherField` was not resolved from the translations like the `field` name. Which is not being fixed

## What's Changed
* fix: add support for vinejs messages provider by getting otherField by @Lupiac in https://github.com/adonisjs/i18n/pull/141

releases.name: Use config provider
releases.description:
The `config/i18n.ts` file must be upgraded as follows 

```diff
import app from '@adonisjs/core/services/app'
import {
  defineConfig,
+ formatters,
+ loaders
} from '@adonisjs/i18n'

export default defineConfig({
  defaultLocale: 'en',
- formatter: 'icu',
+ formatter: formatters.icu(),
  loaders: [
-   {
-     driver: 'fs',
-     location: app.languageFilesPath(),
-   }
+   loaders.fs({
+     location: app.languageFilesPath(),
+   }),
  ],
})
```

## Commits

- refactor: export formatters and loaders from the main entrypoint  34b92c8
- style: format source code  ff059b8
- test: improve coverage  6543eec
- refactor: internals  34da2bc
- refactor: use config providers for resolving config  377daaa
- chore: update dependencies  8628f89

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/i18n/compare/v2.0.0-4...v2.0.0-5

releases.name: Use config providers
releases.description:
The config must be defined using the `services` object as string based drivers are not supported anymore

```diff
import {
  defineConfig,
+ services
} from '@adonisjs/ally'

const allyConfig = defineConfig({
- github: {
-  driver: 'github',
-  clientId: env.get('GITHUB_CLIENT_ID'),
-  clientSecret: env.get('GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET'),
-  callbackUrl: '',
- },
+ github: services.github({
+  clientId: env.get('GITHUB_CLIENT_ID'),
+  clientSecret: env.get('GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET'),
+  callbackUrl: '',
+ }),
})
```

## Commits

- refactor: fix typing issues  a73d9ec
- refactor: move to config providers and remove drivers collection  c1e0bc1
- chore: update dependencies  c9b5a5c

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/ally/compare/v5.0.0-5...v5.0.0-6

releases.name: Add facebook driver and allow email and avatarUrl to be null
releases.description:
## Breaking change

As we are adding new drivers, the possibility of email not being available is increasing. Hence, we must update the `AllyUserContract` to have email as `null` as well.

This does require a small change in your application codebase and you must guard yourself for missing email

```ts
const facebookUser = await ally.use('facebook').user()

if (!facebookUser.email) {
  // handle the use case
}
```

## Commits

- refactor: allow email and avatarUrl to be null  1fd9aa4
- refactor: small improvements to the facebook driver  536230f

releases.name: Prevent path traversal during file uploads
releases.description:
# [11.0.0-next.6](https://github.com/adonisjs/bodyparser/compare/v11.0.0-next.5...v11.0.0-next.6) (2026-01-02)

Security update for **CVE-2026-21440**  (https://github.com/adonisjs/core/security/advisories/GHSA-gvq6-hvvp-h34h)


This patch release fixes a **security issue in multipart file uploads** related to how filenames were handled when moving uploaded files to disk.

The `MultipartFile.move(location)` no longer uses the client-provided filename by default. Previously, if no filename was explicitly provided, the original filename sent by the client was used. This behavior has been changed and now we use `uuid` to create a unique random name for the file.

### Breaking change
If your application relied on the original client filename being preserved implicitly, this behavior has changed. The newly generated file names will now use `uuid`.

**However, if you were always passing an explicit name, then you are unaffected with this breaking change.**

We consider this a necessary breaking change to close a security gap and align with secure defaults. Shipping this as a patch release ensures the fix reaches all users, including those who do not regularly upgrade major versions.

releases.name: Breaking - Prevent path traversal during file uploads
releases.description:
## [10.1.2](https://github.com/adonisjs/bodyparser/compare/v10.1.1...v10.1.2) (2026-01-02)

Security update for **CVE-2026-21440**  (https://github.com/adonisjs/core/security/advisories/GHSA-gvq6-hvvp-h34h)

This patch release fixes a **security issue in multipart file uploads** related to how filenames were handled when moving uploaded files to disk.

The `MultipartFile.move(location)` no longer uses the client-provided filename by default. Previously, if no filename was explicitly provided, the original filename sent by the client was used. This behavior has been changed and now we use `uuid` to create a unique random name for the file.

### Breaking change
If your application relied on the original client filename being preserved implicitly, this behavior has changed. The newly generated file names will now use `uuid`.

**However, if you were always passing an explicit name, then you are unaffected with this breaking change.**

We consider this a necessary breaking change to close a security gap and align with secure defaults. Shipping this as a patch release ensures the fix reaches all users, including those who do not regularly upgrade major versions.

### Bug Fixes

* path traversal during file.move operation ([6795c0e](https://github.com/adonisjs/bodyparser/commit/6795c0e3fa824ae275bbd992aae60609e96f0f03))

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/bodyparser/compare/v10.1.1...v10.1.2

releases.name: Fix issue with uploading zip files
releases.description:
# [11.0.0-next.3](/compare/v11.0.0-next.2...v11.0.0-next.3) (2025-11-20)

There were two issues in place. 

- The `file-type` package was failing with detecting the mime-type of zip files and it was throwing an error
- The bodyparser was not aborting the request and instead processed the entire stream. As a result of this, the file was uploaded successfully, but with a series of error logs on the console.

### Bug Fixes

* abort when partHandler.reportProgress throws an error 108a3df

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/bodyparser/compare/v11.0.0-next.2...v11.0.0-next.3

releases.name: Allow merging multipart fields and files
releases.description:
# [10.1.0](https://github.com/adonisjs/bodyparser/compare/v10.0.3...v10.1.0) (2025-05-15)

The multipart fields and files are kept as two separate objects by the bodyparser. This creates an issue when you have nested data and want to keep some files and fields next to each other. For example, you are creating a set of users using a batch request, and each user could have their avatar next to them.

```
users[0].name
users[0].email
users[0].avatar

users[1].name
users[1].email
users[1].avatar
```

```ts
request.all() // [{ name: 'foo', email: '...' }, { name: 'bar', email: '...' }]
request.allFiles() // [{  avatar: MultipartFile }, { avatar: MultipartFile }]
```

Now, when you decide to validate the `users` array. AdonisJS will perform a shallow comparison of `fields` and `files`, and the `files` array will override the `fields`, hence the `name` and the `email` properties will be missing in the final merged object.

```ts
{
  ...request.all(),
 ...request.allFiles(),
}
```

One way to fix this issue is to perform a deep merge. However, instead of performing a deep merge after the fact, we can make the bodyparser collect all the data in one object called the request body.

This is what we do now (after this release). However, we do it behind a feature flag because it is technically a breaking change, even though it should not impact most applications.

However, if you are looping through the request body (accessed via `request.all()`, you will have to be a bit careful and account for some values to be an instance of `MultipartFile`.

### Opting into the new behavior

You can opt into the new behavior by defining the following feature flag inside the `adonisrc.ts` file.

```ts
{
  experimental: {
    mergeMultipartFieldsAndFiles: true,
  }
}
```

### Features

* merge multipart fields and files when "mergeMultipartFieldsAndFiles" flag is enabled ([f03e53e](https://github.com/adonisjs/bodyparser/commit/f03e53e6a8a8c7e2c0501286c1ecc4d06d07f3e3))

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/bodyparser/compare/v10.0.3...v10.1.0

releases.name: Preparing for v6
releases.description:
## Breaking changes

- ESM only
- The exports have been re-arranged and uses Node.js subpath exports.
- The `file.moveToDisk` method has been removed. The same method will be added by drive package. Earlier, it was mandatory to use `drive` in order to use `bodyparser`. However, after this release, drive is optional.
- Removed `queryString` config option from raw bodyparser config block. The option was unused and hence has no breaking change at runtime, but will give static type error.
- The `BodyParserMiddleware` class accepts the bodyParser config as the constructor argument. Earlier, it used to accept AdonisJS config service.

## Commits
- refactor: remove unused queryString option from raw body parser  edb024e
- chore: rename files in git index  cb73e5c
- chore: rename files in git index  15a47a4
- docs: update README and remove old changelog file  c7babbe
- test: improve tests coverage  7068e39
- chore: add debug statements  fd51175
- feat: make MultipartFile macroable to make it extensible  67cbf5f
- refactor: refactor codebase for v6  43946d1

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/bodyparser/compare/v8.1.7...v9.0.0-0

releases.name: Session Collection
releases.description:
# Session Tagging & SessionCollection

This release adds session tagging support and a new SessionCollection API for programmatic session management.

## New Features

### Session Tagging

Link sessions to user IDs, enabling features like "Logout from all devices" or displaying active sessions in account settings.
```ts
// Tag the current session after login
await session.tag(String(user.id))
```

### SessionCollection API
Manage sessions programmatically outside of HTTP requests:

```ts
import { SessionCollection } from '@adonisjs/session'

const sessionCollection = await app.container.make(SessionCollection)

await sessionCollection.get(sessionId)           // Get session data
await sessionCollection.destroy(sessionId)       // Destroy a session
await sessionCollection.tag(sessionId, userId)   // Tag a session
await sessionCollection.tagged(userId)           // Get all sessions for a user
```

### Supported Stores

Session tagging is supported by redis, database, and memory stores only.

### Database Migration

If using the database store, run node ace make:session-table for new projects, or add the user_id column to existing sessions tables:

```ts
table.string('user_id').nullable().index()
```


## Commits

* add support for tagging + SessionCollection ([#94](https://github.com/adonisjs/session/issues/94)) ([a51e388](https://github.com/adonisjs/session/commit/a51e3888a41face31514dd66f7650fe85da31cef))

releases.name: Flash message changes and target v7
releases.description:
# [8.0.0-next.0](https://github.com/adonisjs/session/compare/v7.5.1...v8.0.0-next.0) (2025-09-09)

In this release, we remove the deprecated `errors` property from the flash messages and you must rely on the `inputErrors` property instead to read input validation errors. Also, for Inertia applications, we no longer flash input data

### Bug Fixes

* pin swc as it is broken ([abe3770](https://github.com/adonisjs/session/commit/abe3770c6e093d05f3f99b8cef8f531873ffdd8b))


### Features

* do not flash input data for inertia requests ([91b60ee](https://github.com/adonisjs/session/commit/91b60ee7396b3be1b8711288dbc3236bdc628f49)), closes [#89](https://github.com/adonisjs/session/issues/89)


### BREAKING CHANGES

* Remove deprecated errors property from the flash message
and instead rely on inputErrors property

releases.name: Breaking change in config
releases.description:
The configuration now uses the concept of stores and configures them using the `stores` object. This release only impacts the users using the alpha release.

```diff
import {
  defineConfig,
+ stores
} from '@adonisjs/session'

export default defineConfig({
  // ...rest of the config remains unchanged.

- driver: env.get('SESSION_DRIVER'),
+ store: env.get('SESSION_DRIVER'),

- file: {},
- redis: {},

+ stores: {
+   cookie: stores.cookie(),
+ },
})

```

- refactor: use session stores and get rid of drivers collection  3e967ac

## Breaking changes 

- Package is now ESM only
- We used to use Server hooks to initiate and terminate sessions. It was invisible to the end user. Now you'll need to include a middleware in your `kernel.ts` file :
	```ts
	import server from '@adonisjs/core/services/server'
	
	server.use([
	  // ...
	  () => import('@adonisjs/session/session_middleware'),
	])
	```
	Indirectly, this will also allow more flexibility on the routes on which you wish to activate sessions. ( Related : https://github.com/adonisjs/rfcs/pull/29 )
- The function for defining config is now called `defineConfig`
- Types are now available from `./types` export

## Other changes
- Empty sessions are now deleted to optimise storage space. See https://github.com/adonisjs/session/pull/78

## Commits

- chore: add engines.node  327488b
- chore: add c8 as dev dep  92dfe44

- chore: export bindings types from root  24ff390

releases.name: Optionally read CSRF exception error message via i18n
releases.description:
This release adds support for reading CSRF error message from i18n integration. You can define the translation message for CSRF errors inside the `errors.json` file as follows.

```json
{
  "E_BAD_CSRF_TOKEN": "Session expired"
}
```

Also, fixed the bug to include input data when sending CSRF error via flash messages

releases.name: Improvements to file.moveToDisk method
Introduce readThenWrite option when moving multipart files
releases.description:
# [3.4.0](https://github.com/adonisjs/drive/compare/v3.3.0...v3.4.0) (2025-03-19)


### Features

* introduce readThenWrite action ([0304b2c](https://github.com/adonisjs/drive/commit/0304b2c6b97e7085941efae3b89466737b544020))

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/drive/compare/v3.3.0...v3.4.0

releases.name: Bug fixes and allow moving files to a disk using different actions
releases.description:
Earlier the `moveToDisk` method did not mark the file as moved, which resulted in not having access to the `fileName` and the `filePath` properties. That has been fixed now.

Also, we have exposed another option named `moveAs` to decide how to copy the file from the `tmpPath` to the remote disk. The action could be.

- `stream`: Perform a move operation via `disk.moveFromFs` method https://flydrive.dev/docs/disk_api#movefromfs
- `buffer`: Create a new remote object using `disk.put` method. This method will first read the file contents into a Buffer and then write it to the destination. This is the only method that works with SupaBase storage

# [3.3.0](https://github.com/adonisjs/drive/compare/v3.2.0...v3.3.0) (2025-03-19)


### Bug Fixes

* handle writeOptions when diskNameOrOptions is provided ([#55](https://github.com/adonisjs/drive/issues/55)) ([cbc7601](https://github.com/adonisjs/drive/commit/cbc7601de2bc48b7aa3f16ec10a1c25b035ad31b))
* mark file as moved after moving it via moveToDisk method ([e569c11](https://github.com/adonisjs/drive/commit/e569c1166e409a80627dbdfe4028716b9c75e3dd))


### Features

* allow streaming file via moveToDisk ([8209dfa](https://github.com/adonisjs/drive/commit/8209dfae5d03ea9ad2b031fac92a1578d4d03850)), closes [#56](https://github.com/adonisjs/drive/issues/56)

## What's Changed
* fix: handle writeOptions when diskNameOrOptions is provided by @kamilcglr in https://github.com/adonisjs/drive/pull/55

## New Contributors
* @kamilcglr made their first contribution in https://github.com/adonisjs/drive/pull/55

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/drive/compare/v3.2.0...v3.3.0

releases.name: Upgrade to work with AdonisJS v6 and Lucid 21
releases.description:
This should be a drop-in upgrade for v6 applications except the changes in the import paths. 

The only breaking change comes in form of implementing custom strategies. Now, strategies do not have to implement the `makeSlug` method and only implement the `makeSlugUnique` method. Also, the `makeSlugUnique` method now receives an instance of the model instead of the model class.

# [3.0.0](https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid-slugify/compare/v3.0.0-beta.0...v3.0.0) (2025-03-09)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid-slugify/compare/v2.2.1...v3.0.0

releases.name: Beta release targeting Lucid 21
releases.description:
# [3.0.0-beta.0](https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid-slugify/compare/v2.2.1...v3.0.0-beta.0) (2025-03-09)


### Features

* allow replacing slugify method ([3b43e4a](https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid-slugify/commit/3b43e4a6f5e6e5c290765089c4650c563d83e565))
* finish implementation ([c5e12af](https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid-slugify/commit/c5e12afc049644f8fd6c9943d2f701306919eb0a))
* setup repo ([21e7593](https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid-slugify/commit/21e7593768992d2b46051bf45cbd0d14e96edd0c))
* start from scratch ([ae71503](https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid-slugify/commit/ae71503a909dc4a40f8a541afc2be4f594de9af4))

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/lucid-slugify/compare/v2.2.1...v3.0.0-beta.0

releases.name: Introduce defineIdentifier and defineIdentifierIfMissing methods
releases.description:
The `Env.identifier` method has been deprecated in favor of `defineIdentifier` and  `defineIdentifierIfMissing` method is added to define the identifier only when its not already defined

## 6.2.0 (2025-03-19)

* test: small improvements to the test cases ([f1e786b](https://github.com/adonisjs/env/commit/f1e786b))
* test: update test to have assertions ([459aece](https://github.com/adonisjs/env/commit/459aece))

releases.name: Empty Example value
releases.description:
## Changes

Added a third argument `withEmptyExampleValue` to the `EnvEditor` `add` method which allows to insert a empty value in the .env.example file

```ts
const editor = await EnvEditor.create(fs.baseUrl)
editor.add('SECRET_VALUE', 'key, true)
```

This will result in `SECRET_VALUE=key` in the `.env` file, but with `SECRET_VALUE=` in the .env.example file.

## Commits

* Merge pull request #39 from adonisjs/feat/empty-example (418e443)
* chore: wip (9168360)
* chore: readme (eae8a30)
* feat: `add` with empty example value (ddaa57a)

releases.name: Allow to add identifiers
releases.description:
This release adds the possibility to define `identifier`.
The identifier is a string that prefix the environment variable value and let you customize the value resolution.

```ts
import { readFile } from 'node:fs/promises'
import { EnvParser } from '@adonisjs/env'

EnvParser.identifier('file', (value) => {
  return readFile(value, 'utf-8')
})

const envParser = new EnvParser(`
  DB_PASSWORD=file:/run/secret/db_password
`)

console.log(await envParser.parse()) // { DB_PASSWORD: 'Value from file /run/secret/db_password' }
```

## Breaking

The `parse` method of the `EnvParser` class is now async.

## Commits


- Merge pull request #37 from adonisjs/feat/identifier  e7574df
- feat(parser): allow to escape identifier  fbba16b
- test(parser): ensure identifier can be escaped  b5b63fa
- fix(parser): ensure identifier fully-match before using it  821ef92
- feat(parser): allow to add identifiers  0e612ae

https://github.com/adonisjs/env/compare/v5.0.1...v6.0.0

releases.name: Rewrite and ESM only
releases.description:
Following are breaking changes post this release.

- The package is now ESM only.
- The `envLoader` function has been removed. Instead use `EnvLoader` class. 
    ```ts
    // Earlier
    import { envLoader } from '@adonisjs/env'
    const result = envLoader(__dirname)
    ```

    ```ts
    // Now
    import { EnvLoader } from '@adonisjs/env'
    const loader = new EnvLoader(new URL('./', import.meta.url))

    const result = loader.load()
    ```

- The return value of `loader.load` has been changed as well. The `currentEnvContents` is the content of the `.env.[NODE_ENV]` file. Earlier we used to load this file only in test environment. Now, we load this file in all the environments.
  ```ts
  // Earlier
  envLoader(__dirname)
  // Returns - { envContents: string, testEnvContent: string }
  ```

  ```ts
  // Now
  loader.load()
  // Returns - { envContents: string, currentEnvContents: string }
  ```

- The `Env` class no longer process the environment variables. You need to construct the class with processed values. Check README file for the same.
- The `Env.rules` and the `Env.schema` properties are static now. **This is the only breaking change for AdonisJS app, but we can fix this with a codemod.**

## Commits

- refactor: rewrite and ESM only  06a3ac5
- chore: update dependencies  ad2f14d
- refactor: extract `@poppinss/validator-lite` (#31)  9b4e0a9
- chore: update dependencies  8f29e1c
- chore: update dependencies (#30)  58fca6f

## PRs
* chore: update dependencies by @targos in https://github.com/adonisjs/env/pull/30
* refactor: extract `@poppinss/validator-lite` by @Julien-R44 in https://github.com/adonisjs/env/pull/31

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/env/compare/v3.0.9...v4.0.0-0

releases.name: Upgrade to Pino 8 and rewrite in ESM
releases.description:
## Breaking changes

- The package is re-rewritten to work with ESM projects only.
- Removed the concept of `FakeLogger`. AdonisJS apps never used the fake logger. It was mainly for package development.
- Upgraded to `pino@8`. Pino 7 and above has changed the way transports work and also the `pretty` option in config is no longer valid. 

The logging API is still the same and has zero breaking changes :)

## Commits
- docs: do not center labels  7c14c97
- docs(README): update  adb8679
- refactor: export targets helper from the main module  402a426
- refactor: rewrite in ESM with support for multiple managed loggers  c4e317c

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/logger/compare/v4.1.5...v5.0.0-0

releases.name: Support for platform native Response class and breaking changes
releases.description:
# [8.0.0-next.13](/compare/v8.0.0-next.12...v8.0.0-next.13) (2025-12-02)

## Breaking Changes

### Renamed `Request` and `Response` classes

**Migration required:** The `Request` and `Response` classes have been renamed to `HttpRequest` and `HttpResponse` to avoid conflicts with platform-native global classes.

**What you need to do:**

Update your imports and type annotations:
```ts
// Before
import { Request, Response } from '@adonisjs/core/http'

// After
import { HttpRequest, HttpResponse } from '@adonisjs/core/http'
```

**Why this change?** Modern JavaScript runtimes expose native `Request` and `Response` classes globally, which created naming conflicts and confusion. This rename provides better clarity and prevents ambiguity between AdonisJS classes and platform-native APIs.

## New Features

### Platform-native Response support

You can now return platform-native `Response` objects directly via the `response.send()` method. This enables seamless integration with libraries that return standard `Response` objects, such as Vercel's AI SDK.

```typescript
import { streamText } from 'ai'

async handle({ response }: HttpContext) {
  const result = await streamText({
    model: openai('gpt-4'),
    prompt: 'Write a story'
  })
  
  return result.toUIMessageStreamResponse()
}
```

### Custom response serializers

You can now define custom serializers to control how response data is serialized before being sent to the client. This is particularly useful for:

- Adding custom serialization logic for specific data types
- Implementing organization-wide response formatting standards
- Handling special object types

**Configuration:**
```typescript
// config/app.ts
{
  http: definedConfig({
    serializeJSON: (value) => {
      return JSON.stringify(value)
    }
  })
}
```

## Bug Fixes

* Ensure x-request-id header is always set on responses (#111) 5566086, closes #111
* pending references of Response class ab5694a

## Commits

* add support for handling platform native Response and rename our classes d5d971a
* add support for using a custom JSON serializer for outgoing response aebbdc3
* migrate to cookie-es 30533b3
* throw proper error when url builder does not receive routes in correct shape d8d9efa

## PRS
* Ensure x-request-id header is always set on responses by @ThisIsMissEm in https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/pull/111

## New Contributors
* @ThisIsMissEm made their first contribution in https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/pull/111

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/compare/v8.0.0-next.12...v8.0.0-next.13

releases.description:
# [8.0.0-next.0](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/compare/v7.6.1...v8.0.0-next.0) (2025-07-30)


### Bug Fixes

* accidental removal of safeStringify ([1dcbc57](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/1dcbc57ef8626221eaa80f353a38663649d05c6a))
* decodeURI unicode values in the URL, including route params and query string ([f0c045d](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/f0c045d8f8c4ed3e7123f30a1e0bf269a94eeb13)), closes [#105](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/issues/105)

### Features

* add encoreUrl helper ([257af47](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/257af472d106cd6b72df2bc43cc7bb8bea693080))
* add matchRoute and parseRoute helpers ([0114df2](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/0114df2a75aeb456a5b89b5ac03c1d1563854f96))
* add routeInfo and middlewareInfo helpers ([bb83bde](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/bb83bdefbf2130385894fbee98a86ff3f3e4f964))
* add serializeCookie helper ([3952cfc](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/3952cfcbb4844b7c22f7844ab00c0868767c8c26))
* add type-safe heleprs for creating URLs from routes and patterns ([d4558a5](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/d4558a5b5b71f1ea14739d8463ee63cf86f7dfa7))
* create proper class for new URLBuilder and make brisk route and redirect APIs type-safe ([623fd0c](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/623fd0cbafcd9515277830ae4e300a8760b16fc0))
* do not render status page for non-HTML requests ([f205126](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/f205126351d94062292901508a6a9a8d532769bc)), closes [#100](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/issues/100)
* export mime-types package via helpers ([eb5d0af](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/eb5d0af275f34f8f2c7d66336d44bd5b6a4c8561))
* first version of generating the URL builder client ([7aa48d9](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/7aa48d9474fb6f8ddae918f1df9d023093f8d086))
* introduce a new type safe URLBuilder with the ability to generate types ([6181a50](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/6181a509a61c22861cbc1b7ab1fab4724b45e14a))
* introduce config.createRequestId method and remove usage of cuid2 ([4e19034](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/4e190347fa4757d8c4310c210ac91d2edc33bb5c))
* introduce tracing channels ([44e272f](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/44e272f149f5c73ebf8b2420433c520e33d91ff1))
* move tokens to route JSON output ([f2cd82d](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/f2cd82d9abc422c3ecf4a587be73584052219597))
* remove StoreRouteNode in favor of RouteJSON ([c091c13](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/c091c1355dcf13c87aa3b8491b65ae2fc3069782))
* restructure code for client generation and create client build ([7bdc4b6](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/7bdc4b65fae4c80f6695028796c24d5a664e4323))
* speed up response serialization ([36ef522](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/36ef5224a2ca8cdb76619bbe5d4fc0fa30b8df2e))
* store references for all middlewares ([ed3ef29](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/ed3ef2989a006c69d30572ef0ed1024d2cc3e897))
* trace non-stream response serialization ([4069c2d](https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/commit/4069c2db149637ed2a6840f95b57cd90e535d544))


### BREAKING CHANGES

* The request.parsedUrl property is no longer of type UrlWithStringQuery, since
url.parse method in Node.js has been deprecated. Instead, we use a custom implementation to
decode the URI and split the query string from it.

* Router.match method now accepts an additional 3rd argument. Which is a boolean flag to tell if
it should attempt to decode unicode values in the route params.

* The exception handler will no longer consider status pages
when an API request is sent with the Accept header not accept HTML response

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/compare/v7.6.1...v8.0.0-next.0

releases.name: Use status pages return value and add new cookie options
releases.description:
This release fixes the behavior of statusPages property defined on a exception handler. Earlier, a status page callback function was responsible for writing the HTTP response via the `response.send` method. However, how you can return a string value from this method and the will be written as the response.

Also, we now support `partitioned` and `priority` cookie options. Lear more about them here. https://github.com/jshttp/cookie?tab=readme-ov-file#partitioned

## Commits

- fix: use status page return value as response  11324f0
- feat: optionally accept partitioned and priority cookie options  f1397c8
- chore: update dependencies  ea56755

releases.name: Add support for handling validation error messages
releases.description:
The `ExceptionHandler` class now contains special logic to handle validation errors and convert them to HTTP response using content negotiation.

The validation error message can come from any validation library as long as it has `code` and `messages` properties as per the following guidelines.

```ts
// Error code should be set
error.code = 'E_VALIDATION_ERROR'

// Error messages must be an array
error.messages = [
  {
     message: '',
     field: '',
     rule: ''
  }
]
``` 

## Commits

- test: improve assertions to work cross platform  1219602
- chore: add missing dependency required for tests  2cd997e
- feat(ExceptionHandler): separately hand validation error messages  76f6985
- style: format source code  ca7e409
- chore: update dependencies  07020ce

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/http-server/compare/v6.8.2-8...v6.8.2-9

releases.description:
# [9.0.0-next.0](https://github.com/adonisjs/application/compare/v8.4.1...v9.0.0-next.0) (2025-06-10)

- Remove `unstable_assembler` property. Was deprecated a long time back.
- Remove `assetsBundler` property. It's not used for a long time because of bundled Vite integration.
- Rename assembler hooks names. They are now defined within the `@adonisjs/assembler` package and sourced directly from the package. See https://github.com/adonisjs/assembler/releases/tag/v8.0.0-next.0
- Remove `shutdownInReverseOrder` experimental flag. As shutdown hooks are now always executed in reverse order.

### Features

* cleanup assembler hooks related code ([a656453](https://github.com/adonisjs/application/commit/a6564537b5bde79db0297fd8b25375ff44a2efe4))
* remove shutdownInReverseOrder experimental flag, as it is the default behavior ([4e6304c](https://github.com/adonisjs/application/commit/4e6304c142f10cd101c897070a872fbbda5b7f87))


### BREAKING CHANGES

* Assembler hooks are now defined inside the assembler package and the application
package just exposes its types as it is. Also, the hooks have been renamed

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/application/compare/v8.4.1...v9.0.0-next.0

releases.description:
## Changes

The `unstable_assembler` configuration is now `hooks`, see the previous release. 

This release also adds backward compatibility for `unstable_assembler`, which is now deprecated, and will be removed in a future release.

## Commits

* refactor: keep supporting `unstable_assembler` for now (ea767dd)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/application/compare/v8.3.0...v8.3.1

releases.name: Stable assembler hooks
releases.description:
## Changes

We're stabilising the API for assembler hooks. A few breaking changes :

- The RC file property is no longer `unstable_assembler` but `hooks`. You'll need to make this change in your `adonisrc.ts` file
```diff
defineConfig({
- unstable_assembler: {
+ hooks: {
   onBuildStarting: [() => import(‘@adonisjs/vite/build_hook’)]
  }
})
```

- The `onHttpServerMessage` hook has been removed as it is no longer used. This property was never documented.
- The `runner` property has also been removed but has never been used or documented.

## Commits

* chore: update dependencies (4f7443e)
* refactor!: remove onHttpServerMessage hook (c8f7878)
* refactor!: rename from `unstable_assembler` to `hooks` (02d4ef1)
* feat: add new `process.send` parameter (d3e1662)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/application/compare/v8.2.2...v8.3.0

releases.description:

releases.name: Assembler configuration
releases.description:
## Changes

A new configuration section has been added for the Assembler in the RC file. **currently unstable, so use at your own risk: breakings changes are probable**

We can use it to configure two things : 

- A "runner". The runner is the command that will be executed to launch your dev server and your tests.
- Several hooks, such as `onBuildStarting`, `onSourceFileChanged`, `onDevServerStarted`, `onBuildCompleted`, which will allow you to execute code at certain times. 

For example: 

```ts
import { defineConfig } from '@adonisjs/core/app'

export default defineConfig({
  unstable_assembler: {
    runner: {
      name: 'bun',
      command: 'bun',
      args: [],
    },

    onBuildStarting: [
      () => import('@adonisjs/vite/build_hook')
    ],
  },
})
```

## What's Changed
* feat: allow false value for assetsBundler by @Julien-R44 in https://github.com/adonisjs/application/pull/31
* feat: add assembler configuration (unstable) by @Julien-R44 in https://github.com/adonisjs/application/pull/32


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/application/compare/v8.0.4...v8.1.0

releases.name: Breaking change to our stubs system
releases.description: This release changes they way we export variables from a stub. Earlier, we were using YAML frontmatter to export variables from a stub. However, I had a realization that using YAML for this simple task was an overkill. Also, the `js-yaml` package imports `esprima (a JavaScript AST parser)`. Basically, we had a full-blown JS + YAML parser in place to export a couple of variables from stubs. ``` --- to: {{ app.makePath('app/controllers/foo.ts') }} --- ``` In this release, we get rid of YAML parsing altogether and use a simple function to define exports. For example: ``` {{{ exports({ to: app.makePath('app/controllers/foo.ts') }) }}} ``` As a result, processing stubs is faster and the bundle size is small. I agree, bundle size is not an issue, but why pack bloat, when you do not need it :) ## Difference in bundle size **Earlier** **Now** ## Commits - refactor: get rid of the front matter and use a dedicated exports function 62cdaf8 - fix: escape frontmatter json before parsing it 0447130 - refactor: split contents by windows and unix new lines 38245ec - ci: add log messages to debug ci issues b8b96e6 - refactor: use EOL when parsing front-matter 7340791 - ci: fix failing test in ci 0b7c191 - ci: fix failing test in ci 8d16b67 - input: refactor: remove front-matter package in favor of custom,simple implementation c38ba2c - refactor: rename exceptions.ts to errors.ts 5f238ec - chore: use tsup for bundling 31ec5b7 - chore: update dependencies c94637d **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/application/compare/v7.1.2-16...v8.0.0-0

releases.name: Breaking changes to the Events buffer API
releases.description:
Events buffer is created when you call `emitter.fake` and use the output for writing assertions. This release pushes a breaking changes to the Events buffer API.

## Changes to the API you find an emitted event

The `events.exists`, `events.find`, `events.assertEmitted`, `events.assertNotEmitted` methods now always accepts the event name or the class constructor as the first argument. An optional callback can be used to further narrow find the events. 

```diff
const events = emitter.fake()

// Earlier
events.find('new:user')
events.find(NewUser)
- events.find(({ event, data }) => {
-   return event == NewUser && data.user.id === 1
- })

// Now
events.find('new:user')
events.find(NewUser)
+ events.find(NewUser, ({ event, data }) => {
+   return data.user.id === 1
+ })
```

```diff
const events = emitter.fake()

// Earlier
events.assertEmitted(NewUser)
- events.assertEmitted(({ event, data }) => {
-   return event == NewUser && data.user.id === 1
- })

// Now
events.assertEmitted(NewUser)
+ events.assertEmitted(NewUser, ({ event, data }) => {
+   return data.user.id === 1
+ })
```

## Other changes/additions

- Removed `events.filter` method.
- Added `events.assertEmittedCount` method.

## Commits

- refactor: events buffer API  6a0aaed
- chore: publish source maps and use tsc for generating types  12d474c
- chore: update dependencies  2a72a5d

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/events/compare/v9.0.0-0...v9.0.0-1

releases.name: Refactor to ESM and re-organize imports
releases.description:
## Breaking changes

- Remove `config.ts` file. Instead use `defineConfig` method from the package entry point.
- Remove `standalone.ts` file. The package entry point has all the exports.
- Remove `providers`. AdonisJS core will be registering hash in the container.
- Remove `isFaked` property from the hash manager. The property has no use case and was neither documented.
- The `Hash.extend` method now only receives the config the driver should be using. Earlier it used to receive the instance of Hash manager and the mapping name as well. 
   ```ts
   // Earlier
   Hash.extend('md5', (manager, mappingName, config) => {
     console.log(manager === Hash) // true
   })

   // Now
   Hash.extend('md5', (config) => {
   })
   ```
- The types are exported from a sub-path `adonisjs/hash/types`.

## Commits
- ci: normalize test import path for windows  739fd7d
- docs(README): update docs  ac3fe3b
- refactor: rewrite in esm  30e3da6

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/hash/compare/v7.2.2...v8.0.0-0

releases.name: Rewrite for improved security
releases.description:
This release doesn't change the API, but changes the way values are encrypted or signed. The change is made in the favor of improved security and more stable internals.

## Removing Node Simple Encryptor Dependency

Earlier we were using `node-simple-encryptor` npm package to encrypt and decrypt values. The package works great, but has couple of shortcomings.

- The encrypted value has `hmac + iv + encryption digest`. However, there is no separator in between. The package relies on the length of `hmac` and `iv` for locating them inside the final string. However, now we concatenate them with a `dot (.)` separator. The dot separate convention is borrowed from JWT spec.
- It uses `JSON.parse` for converting the verified payload to an object. However, JSON.parse is vulnerable to JSON poisoning.

## No way of verifying encryption purpose

Rails has this interesting concept in which you can encrypt/decrypt values with a purpose. If the encryption purpose is different from the decryption purpose, then the decryption will fail. 

This feature becomes helpful with cookies. So let's say, you set two cookies `tax=40` and `discount=10`. The end user swaps the value of both the cookies, now since the values are valid, the cookie parser will parse and trust them, however, these are not the original values. In short, it is a problem or cookie swapping (not sure if it is a term).

Here is a rails PR https://github.com/rails/rails/pull/32937. The purpose field is inspired by this PR itself.

releases.name: Rename hooks, remove assets bundler and internal cleanup
releases.description:
# [8.0.0-next.0](https://github.com/adonisjs/assembler/compare/v7.8.2...v8.0.0-next.0) (2025-06-02)

## Breaking changes

- In this release we have renamed the following hooks and added a few new one's.
    ```diff
    - onSourceFileChanged
    + fileChanged

    - onDevServerStarted
    + devServerStarted

    - onBuildCompleted
    + buildFinished

    - onBuildStarting
    + buildStarting

    + fileAdded
    + fileRemoved
    + devServerStarting
    + testsStarting
    + testsFinished
    ```
- Support Node 24 and above
- Use `@poppinss/ts-exec` instead of `ts-node`
- Remove concept of assets bundler in favor of inbuilt integration with Vite


### Bug Fixes

* use unix slash for index file paths and names ([bd82fb7](https://github.com/adonisjs/assembler/commit/bd82fb72e6cdccebfd93eaebe032addbae8d564c))


### Features

* add makeEntityIndex to code_transformer ([0548b3f](https://github.com/adonisjs/assembler/commit/0548b3f90753bd86655ca8025249cf4049352611))
* cleanup internals, use ts-exec for running typescript code and new watcher setup ([18c3bb0](https://github.com/adonisjs/assembler/commit/18c3bb0bee2e5b5f96f275e5c5fc3c9333c21429))
* handle hot-hook process messages ([21bcddc](https://github.com/adonisjs/assembler/commit/21bcddc79864acdfde99b8edc892d8782e40d3ab))
* remove assets bundler related code ([a60897b](https://github.com/adonisjs/assembler/commit/a60897b4ef9d45849854be1cd8c48ac286de5631))


### BREAKING CHANGES

* Targets Node 24 and uses ts-exec instead of ts-node

## What's Changed
* Refine Wording When Copying ace.js by @marcuspoehls in https://github.com/adonisjs/assembler/pull/81

## New Contributors
* @marcuspoehls made their first contribution in https://github.com/adonisjs/assembler/pull/81

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/adonisjs/assembler/compare/v7.8.2...v8.0.0-next.0

releases.name: Hooks and Codemods
releases.description:
## Changes

This version adds several new features: 

- An `addVitePlugin` method in the Codemods API that can be used by package creators to add a new Vite plugin to the end-user configuration
- Makes the `project` property of the codemods api public. This allows package authors to have direct access to ts-morph when they need to perform more specific modifications in the end-user code.
- Added bundler and dev server hooks. See #75

releases.name: Rewrite builded Ace file
releases.description:
During the build process, we now rewrite **from-scratch** the `ace.js` file included in the `build` folder so as to remove all references to `ts-node`. That way, you can now run `node ace my-command` in your build project without having to install `ts-node`. 

See this issue that explains the problem : https://github.com/adonisjs/core/issues/4361

> [!WARNING]
> Any modifications to the `ace.js` file will be lost during the build process since the file is rewritten from scratch. If you want to have any additional code that runs before Ace starts, you should instead do it inside the `bin/console.ts` file.

## Commits

- test: fix wrong test assertion  adcc145
- refactor: rewrite builded ace file (#67)  a2801ff
- chore: update dependencies  fa322d7

https://github.com/adonisjs/assembler/compare/v7.1.0...v7.1.1

releases.name: Disable experimental warnings
releases.description:
As of today, TSNode exports a loader hook which must be applied using the `--loader` Node.js CLI flag. However, the `--loader` flag was experimental and emits a warning. This release disables that warning for a nicer CLI experience.

With that said, we are waiting on Ts-node to export an import hook that can be applied using the `--import` Node.js CLI flag and then we can safely remove the flags in use to suppress warnings. 

## Commits

- test: fix breaking test  6f28f35
- refactor: disable loader warnings  e74612b
- chore: update dependencies  4744ee1

https://github.com/adonisjs/assembler/compare/v7.0.0...v7.1.0

releases.name: Get ready for V6
releases.description:
Package is now only ESM. Ready to be used with AdonisJS v6 after some polish

## Breaking changes

- ESM only
- Repl constructor signature has changed. It now accepts an object of options instead of individual arguments : 

Before : 
```ts
new Repl(adonisRequireTs, '.history_file_name')
```
Now : 
```ts
new Repl({ compiler, historyFilePath })
```

`compiler` must follow the given interface : 
```ts
interface Compiler {
  compile (contents: string, filename: string): string
  supportsTypescript: boolean
}
```
That means you are not forced to use `@adonisjs/require-ts` anymore. In theory, any TypeScript compiler should work. Tried with `tsc` and `swc` and both worked fine.

- Since Node.js REPL isn't executed in a ESM context, you can't directly use the `import` statement. Instead, all modules should be imported with dynamic imports. For example : 
```ts
// Before
import { foo } from './bar'

// Now
const { foo } = await import('./bar')
```

However, note that you can still use the `import` statement in the file you are importing through dynamic imports. and ESM is fully supported in those files because they are handled by the Node.js loader 

## New features

- Added syntax highlighting in the REPL
- Also added bracket matching. That means when you are hovering a bracket, the matching one will be highlighted.

---

- chore: add c8  428ca07
- chore: update np config  3541a49
- chore: add missing dependency  65bc769
- feat: rewrite for v6  8f18e09
